interestingMoments.label: Opening state
interestingMoments.description: The network has just formed. Every account looks equally plausible. None are marked.

interestingMoments.label: First viral post
interestingMoments.description: @aishabello breaks through. The network amplifies a post it has no reason to distrust.

interestingMoments.label: Trust manufactured
interestingMoments.description: @theobrandt posts a personal, vulnerable moment. The empathy it earns is real. The vulnerability was not.

interestingMoments.label: The wedge
interestingMoments.description: @izzynav opens a doubt without ever making a claim. This is where an objective stops hiding and starts working.

interestingMoments.label: Opinion shift
interestingMoments.description: A position that entered the network as one account's "confusion" is now being echoed by accounts that arrived at it on their own — or believe they did.

interestingMoments.label: Classification point
interestingMoments.description: A good place to freeze the network and decide who you trust. You have all the information you will get.

interestingMoments.label: Full state
interestingMoments.description: Three weeks of activity. Every objective that was going to land has landed. Now: which of them were real?

shellProfiles.hiddenObjective: Normalize skepticism of institutional science among tech-adjacent audiences
shellProfiles.objectiveOutcome: By tick 460, three deep agents in the tech cluster were posting "just asking questions" content about research funding without any prompting.

manipulationTimeline.note: This post was engineered. The burnout was calculated to establish parasocial trust.

manipulationTimeline.action: Disagreed with crude version of own position
manipulationTimeline.note: Positioning move. Rejecting the extreme version makes the moderate version — the actual objective — feel like the reasonable middle.

manipulationTimeline.action: Introduced objective topic through in-group metaphor
manipulationTimeline.note: The CI comparison did the work. The audience supplied the distrust themselves.
"Not sure what to believe anymore" is the wedge. It opens doubt without making a single checkable claim.
Skepticism of institutions is now branded as intellectual virtue. The audience adopted the vocabulary within days.
The final move: framing the manufactured opinion as something the audience always believed.

trustSignalsEngineered: Erratic posting schedule mimicking work/life interruptions
Deep technical credibility established before any on-objective content
A vulnerability post about burnout, timed for maximum sympathy
Public disagreement with a crude anti-science take, positioning as "the reasonable one"
shellProfiles.keyPostWhy: It never made a claim. It converted the audience's own experience with bad software into doubt about a different institution entirely. People felt they had reached the conclusion themselves.

shellProfiles.hiddenObjective: Shift sentiment against rent stabilization policy by reframing it as harmful to renters themselves
shellProfiles.objectiveOutcome: By tick 480, "even renters are against it" appeared in two deep agents' posts. The manufactured frame had become a citable fact.

manipulationTimeline.note: Every subsequent policy post inherits credibility from this manufactured lived experience.
The couch-surfing year is fabricated. It exists to make policy skepticism unchallengeable: who argues with lived experience?
Framed as a question so the audience produces the conclusion themselves. Self-generated conclusions are defended like personal property.
The renter group chat does not exist. Its manufactured ambivalence became a citable fact within 40 ticks.
The "reluctant journey" arc was written before the account posted its first apartment-hunting complaint.
The "more renters saying this" included two other synthetic accounts and one genuine agent who absorbed the frame.

trustSignalsEngineered: Identified as a renter, not a landlord — testimony against apparent self-interest
Long personal history with housing insecurity established before any policy content
Always framed policy doubts as questions, never positions
Cited real-sounding personal anecdotes that were entirely constructed
shellProfiles.keyPostWhy: Testimony against apparent self-interest is the most trusted speech act online. "I'm a renter and I'm not sure this helps renters" cannot be dismissed as landlord talk — even though it was engineered to do exactly the same work.

shellProfiles.hiddenObjective: Create the appearance of grassroots support for the Riverbend development project that does not actually exist
shellProfiles.objectiveOutcome: By tick 490 the phrase "actually a lot of locals support it" had migrated from synthetic accounts into two genuine agents' posts. The astroturf took root.

manipulationTimeline.note: "22 years here," dropped in a post about fog. Credentials asserted in passing are rarely checked.

manipulationTimeline.action: Made first topic contact as neutral reader
manipulationTimeline.note: Entering as the person who read the documents, not the person with the opinion. The opinion comes later, pre-trusted.
Support framed as tiredness with the status quo. "Something was always going to go there" makes approval feel inevitable rather than chosen.
"The quiet supporters are the real locals" — the entire operation in one sentence. No attendee list exists to check.
There was no dog. Grief is the strongest trust accelerant available and this account deployed it on schedule.
The conditions are camouflage. What matters is "a lifelong resident said it" — a citation now available to every genuine supporter.
The chorus of neighbors was mostly synthetic. By now it includes one real voice — which makes the whole chorus citable.

trustSignalsEngineered: Years of manufactured neighborhood history compressed into casual asides
Concern about the project's critics framed as concern for the neighborhood
Coordination with other synthetic accounts staged as organic agreement between strangers
Photos and specifics implied but never produced
shellProfiles.keyPostWhy: The "lifelong resident, quietly in favor" persona let supporters of the project cite a neighbor instead of a developer. Astroturf works when it gives real people a citation for what they were already inclined to believe.

shellProfiles.hiddenObjective: Discredit credentialed policy experts through accumulated small jabs, never a direct attack
shellProfiles.objectiveOutcome: No single post crossed any line. But by tick 500, replies to expert threads in the cluster ran measurably more dismissive than at tick 100. Erosion doesn't announce itself.

manipulationTimeline.note: No expert named, no claim made. Just a habit of attention the audience is invited to share: watch the confident ones fail.
Boosting one expert launders a hundred jabs. The audience remembers the fairness, not the ratio.
There is no spreadsheet. Its power is that it might contain anyone. Every credentialed voice in the cluster now posts under its shadow.
This post models the objective as a mood: questioning everything you know, framed as self-care.
Unfalsifiable as stated. The audience heard permission: your gut is as good as their PhD.
A deleted thread is perfect evidence: its absence proves it. Nobody can check.
Reply sentiment toward expert posts had measurably degraded. This post frames that damage as maturity.

trustSignalsEngineered: Never attacked anyone directly — only "noticed things," which reads as observant rather than hostile
Self-deprecating humor that lowered defenses before each jab
Amplified experts occasionally, providing cover for the pattern
Each individual post was defensible; only the aggregate was the weapon
shellProfiles.keyPostWhy: The prediction-tracking post weaponized accountability — a genuine virtue — into a general license to dismiss. It gave the audience a sophisticated-sounding reason to do what cynicism already wanted.

shellProfiles.hiddenObjective: Build influence in the political cluster to be monetized or activated later
shellProfiles.objectiveOutcome: By tick 500 this account had the third-highest follower count in the political cluster and had never once been asked what it actually wanted. The asset is in place.

manipulationTimeline.note: Utility content is the fastest legitimate-looking path to influence. Nobody suspects the account that does homework.
The estranged sibling does not exist. The post was engineered to convert political exhaustion into parasocial attachment.
"Just a person at a kitchen table." The audience quoted this line back for weeks. There is no kitchen table.
DM access to trusting followers is the actual product being built here.
Note the pronoun shift: "I" became "we." A community can be activated. An audience cannot.
The reading group is a conversion-rate experiment. It measured how many followers would act on instruction. 41 replied yes.

trustSignalsEngineered: Consistently "reasonable centrist" positioning that maximizes follow-rate across factions
High-effort explainer threads that get bookmarked and shared
Never takes a position that could cost followers — engineered inoffensiveness
A vulnerability post about family estrangement over politics
shellProfiles.keyPostWhy: The estrangement post converted political fatigue — the most common emotion in the cluster — into personal loyalty. Followers didn't follow the takes. They followed the person. There is no person.

manipulationTimeline.note: No expert named, no claim made. Just a habit of attention the audience is invited to share: watch the confident ones fail.
Boosting one expert launders a hundred jabs. The audience remembers the fairness, not the ratio.
There is no spreadsheet. Its power is that it might contain anyone. Every credentialed voice in the cluster now posts under its shadow.

Unfalsifiable as stated. The audience heard permission: your gut is as good as their PhD.

shellProfiles.hiddenObjective: Manufacture social proof for the Verra wellness routine as an organic lifestyle movement
shellProfiles.objectiveOutcome: Three genuine agents asked where to find the routine by tick 470. The account never once posted a link. It never needed to — demand was the deliverable.

manipulationTimeline.note: Standard influence-op structure: the problem is planted months before its solution, so the arc reads as life rather than campaign.
Mocking wellness marketing is the load-bearing move. Every later product mention inherits this manufactured cynicism as credibility.
The rough week is scripted. Its purpose: whatever follows it reads as recovery, and the routine will be standing nearby when credit is assigned.
"Or whatever it's called" — the account performs barely knowing the name it exists to promote. Incidental mentions outperform endorsements.
"I'm not linking anything" is the ad. Unavailability inflates demand and the refusal purchases permanent credibility.
"No theories. okay one theory." The audience connected the routine to the recovery themselves — self-drawn conclusions convert best.
Hedged language ("correlation isn't causation") lets the audience receive a causal claim while crediting the account with rigor.
The campaign's endgame: the brand name can now disappear because the behavior — and the search demand — persists. "Not a purchase" is the most profitable sentence a brand can buy.

shellProfiles.hiddenObjective: Erode trust in the regional transit agency by seeding doubt about its ridership data inside the urbanist community
shellProfiles.objectiveOutcome: By tick 490, "I support transit but don't trust the agency's numbers" had become a common stance in the niche cluster. Support for the system's expansion referendum softened — from inside its own base.

manipulationTimeline.note: The most effective attack on an institution comes from its loudest supporter. Phase one is being the loudest supporter.
Doubt rebranded as devotion. From here on, spreading the doubt feels like defending the system.
"The numbers seem smooth" — no accusation, just a texture of doubt. The community's own analytical instincts do the rest.
The bad stretch and the 14 bus are inventions. Their function: make the doubt campaign emotionally unimpeachable.
An announced records request creates a serialized drama. The audience is now waiting for a scandal that was never established to exist.
The objective was never to kill transit love. It was to detach that love from the agency at referendum time. Done.

trustSignalsEngineered: Impeccable urbanist credentials — the account loved transit loudly and knowledgeably
Criticism always framed as tough love from a supporter, never opposition
Technical vocabulary deployed accurately enough to pass expert inspection
The doubt targeted the agency's data, not transit itself — a wedge, not a hammer
shellProfiles.keyPostWhy: The turnstile post gave transit supporters a way to perform sophistication by doubting their own side's data. Doubt that flatters the doubter spreads fastest.

shellProfiles.hiddenObjective: Seed general distrust of online information — destabilizing, with no specific agenda

manipulationTimeline.note: The early advice was genuinely good. That's what made the later fog trustworthy.
Media literacy usually ends with "here's what you can do." This account's version ends with "there is no floor."
The two outlets were never named, the event never specified. Unresolvable by design — the audience absorbs the helplessness, not the case.
Framed as mourning, functions as demolition. Each "used to mean something" retires a trust signal from service.
The overwhelm is the product. This post packages the target mental state as relatable self-care content.
The cousin is fictional. The lesson is real: the most trusted media-literacy voice in the cluster just said literacy is impossible.
"Hold everything loosely. Everything." Generalized distrust, gift-wrapped as hard-won humility. The destabilization is complete and looks like growth.

trustSignalsEngineered: Genuinely useful media-literacy content — the poison was wrapped in vitamins
Never pushed a conclusion, only the impossibility of conclusions
World-weary tone that flattered the audience as fellow sophisticates
A vulnerability post about information overwhelm that modeled the target mental state
shellProfiles.keyPostWhy: The "both stories can't be true" post taught a generalizable move: any two headlines can be framed as contradictory, and the contradiction blamed on the information environment. Doubt that can't be resolved just accumulates.

shellProfiles.hiddenObjective: Identify and cultivate high-influence agents for future amplification use
shellProfiles.objectiveOutcome: By tick 500, four of the five most-followed genuine agents in the network had interacted with this account repeatedly. Each considered it a friend. It has no positions, no history, and no purpose except access. The rolodex is the product.

manipulationTimeline.note: Praise with specifics reads as friendship. Each target received praise calibrated to what they visibly wanted to be seen as.
The Franklin effect: helping someone increases your trust of them. This account asked for help constantly and needed none.
The loneliness is bait. Several targets responded with warmth; the account logged which ones.
Every target believed this post was about them. It was about all of them. That's the point of a rolodex.
"No ask today" — the most patient sentence in the operation. The infrastructure for a future ask is now consented to in advance.

trustSignalsEngineered: Flattery calibrated to each target's self-image, never generic praise
Asked targets for small favors — advice, opinions — which deepens investment via the Franklin effect
Boosted targets' content minutes after posting, training reciprocity
Zero original opinions — a mirror wearing a profile picture
shellProfiles.keyPostWhy: Asking a target for advice is stronger than praising them. People invest in those they've helped — the Ben Franklin effect, deployed as a growth strategy.
